purpose: Parse raw rundown log files from NRCS systems into structured records suitable for prediction and evaluation. Handles format variations, malformed lines, and story segmentation.
components: Log parser: Converts raw text lines into structured Item objects with field-value pairs
Story segmenter: Groups items into stories based on boundary detection rules
Field extractor: Identifies and extracts target field values from parsed items
Data provenance tracker: Records source file, line range, parser version, and modifications for every example
Dataset manifest: Maintains versioned inventory of all datasets with content hashes

purpose: Generate field value predictions for story/item objects using a frozen LLM augmented with dynamic retrieval. This is the core inference layer of the benchmark.
components: Frozen LLM: The base language model used for semantic understanding and generation, with no parameter modifications
Prompt templates: Versioned task descriptions with input/output format specifications, stored separately from code
Example selector: Retrieves relevant few-shot examples from the retrieval index based on input context similarity
Dynamic retriever: Fetches command templates, field definitions, and historical examples at inference time
Keyword expander: Maps natural language keywords to full command structures via template matching
Semantic parser: Converts rundown text into structured command representations with ambiguity resolution
Confidence calibrator: Maps raw model outputs to calibrated confidence scores

purpose: Measure prediction quality using standardized metrics, generate reports, and enforce deployment gates. Provides the scientific evidence base for all benchmark claims.
components: Evaluation harness: Configurable test execution engine supporting single-line and multi-line modes
Metric computer: Calculates F1, MRR, Top-K, exact-match, and bootstrap confidence intervals
Evaluation config: JSON-based configuration specifying mode, target fields, metrics, and dataset splits
Report generator: Produces human-readable and machine-readable evaluation reports
Sabotage test runner: Executes adversarial test suites to detect overclaimed capability
Deployment gate: Enforces minimum metric thresholds for deployability claims

purpose: Capture and process user feedback signals (explicit and implicit) to drive prediction improvement without retraining. Defines policies for signal collection, aggregation, and exploration.
components: Feedback signal collectors: Capture accept, reject, modify, rate actions and implicit behavioral signals
Feedback store: Append-only log of feedback events with prediction context and timestamps
Signal aggregator: Combines multiple feedback signals with recency weighting and conflict resolution
Position bias debiaser: Normalizes implicit signals against display position bias
Exploration engine: Occasionally presents alternative predictions to gather feedback on non-optimal candidates

purpose: Define the integration boundary between the benchmark and production systems, including protocol adapters, plugin interfaces, and deployment gates. All deployment-layer components are mock-only or deferred in Stage A.
components: Mock protocol adapters: Simulate MOS, REST, and TCP interactions without live dependencies
Protocol contracts: Formal specifications of adapter interfaces sufficient for real implementation
Adapter boundary: Clear interface separating internal logic from protocol implementations
Inception plugin interface: Versioned API specification for production system integration (deferred)
Human review schemas: Data structures for suggestion cards and feedback actions (benchmark-only)
Deployment gate enforcer: Verifies that predictions meet minimum quality thresholds before deployment
